seo.title: Data Scientist Resume Examples & ATS Templates 2026
seo.description: Learn how to write a perfect Data Scientist resume that passes the ATS. Discover the top keywords, common mistakes, and view a complete 100/100 resume example.
seo.keywords: data scientist resume, machine learning resume, data science resume template, ats resume
seo.ogTitle: How to Write a Data Scientist Resume in 2026
seo.ogDescription: Steal our exact resume template that landed roles at Google and Meta.

hero.title: Data Scientist Resume Examples
hero.description: Stop getting auto-rejected by the ATS. Here is the exact resume structure, keywords, and action verbs you need to land interviews in 2026.

mistakes: Using colorful templates with columns (ATS systems cannot parse them).
Listing tools without context (e.g., just writing 'Python' instead of 'Used Python to build a churn prediction model').
Failing to quantify impact (e.g., 'Improved accuracy' instead of 'Improved model accuracy by 14%, saving $1.2M annually').
tips: Always place 'Skills' near the top if you are a junior, and 'Experience' near the top if you are senior.
Include a hyperlink to your GitHub and LinkedIn in the header.

exampleResume.name: Sarah Jenkins
exampleResume.title: Data Scientist
exampleResume.summary: Data Scientist with 3+ years of experience building predictive models and data pipelines. Proven track record of leveraging Python and SQL to increase revenue by $2M+ through targeted ML algorithms.

bullets: Developed a random forest churn prediction model in Python (Scikit-Learn), reducing customer churn by 12% in Q3.
Designed and maintained 15+ automated ETL pipelines using SQL and Apache Airflow, processing 500GB of daily data.
Conducted A/B testing on pricing algorithms, resulting in a 4% increase in total revenue.
Built interactive Tableau dashboards utilized by the executive team to monitor KPIs, saving 10 hours of manual reporting weekly.
Optimized slow-running SQL queries, improving database retrieval times by 40%.

exampleResume.skills: Python, R, SQL, Machine Learning (XGBoost, Random Forest), TensorFlow, Tableau, Git, AWS
